conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me first review the official eligibility rules so I can ask you the right questions.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for your patience. I've reviewed the eligibility rules. To assess whether you're eligible for Child Benefit, I need to ask you some questions.

Let me start with: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I'd like to find out if I'm eligible for Child Benefit. I have one child, Alex, who's 17.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. I can see you have one child, Alex, who is 17 years old.

Since Alex is 17, they may still qualify if they're in approved education or training. **Is Alex currently in approved education or training?** (This includes things like A-levels, T-levels, Scottish Highers, NVQ level 3 or below, or certain types of approved training.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex isn't in approved education. But they have started an apprenticeship recently.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. I need to clarify something important: **Where is Alex doing their apprenticeship - is it in England, or in Scotland, Wales, or Northern Ireland?**

This matters because the rules differ depending on the location of the apprenticeship.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: The apprenticeship is in England. We live in Manchester, so that's where Alex is doing it.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. I also need to ask a few more questions to complete the assessment:

**Do you live with Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Just a few more questions to complete the assessment:

**Are you currently living in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - in Manchester, as I mentioned.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you. Just one final question:

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. I now have enough details to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Let me review the situation based on the official rules:

- Alex is 17 years old
- Alex is not in approved education or training
- Alex has started an apprenticeship in England
- You live with Alex in the UK (Manchester)
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex

According to the official guidance, you'll stop getting Child Benefit if your child "starts an apprenticeship in England" when they are no longer in approved education or training. Since Alex has started an apprenticeship in England and is not in approved education or training, you would not be eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I'm sorry to inform you that **you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex**. 

This is because Alex has started an apprenticeship in England. The government rules specifically state that Child Benefit stops when a child starts an apprenticeship in England and is no longer in approved education or training.

If you were previously receiving Child Benefit for Alex, those payments would have stopped when the apprenticeship began. If you have any questions about this decision or need further assistance, you can contact the Child Benefit Office directly.

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 17 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period; Child has started an apprenticeship in England
circumstances: Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is 17 years old and has started an apprenticeship in England. According to the Child Benefit eligibility rules, you stop getting Child Benefit if your child 'starts an apprenticeship in England' when they are no longer in approved education or training. Since Alex is not in approved education or training and has started an apprenticeship in England, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK with their 17-year-old child Alex. While children under 20 can qualify for Child Benefit if they stay in approved education or training, Alex is not in approved education or training and has started an apprenticeship in England. The rules specifically state that Child Benefit stops when a child starts an apprenticeship in England, making the claimant ineligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 17 years old and has started an apprenticeship in England. According to the Child Benefit eligibility rules, you stop getting Child Benefit if your child 'starts an apprenticeship in England' when they are no longer in approved education or training. Since Alex is not in approved education or training and has started an apprenticeship in England, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK with their 17-year-old child Alex. While children under 20 can qualify for Child Benefit if they stay in approved education or training, Alex is not in approved education or training and has started an apprenticeship in England. The rules specifically state that Child Benefit stops when a child starts an apprenticeship in England, making the claimant ineligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
